hero.question: Will Arizona win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 27.6%, the crowd is pricing Arizona as a fringe Final Four contender, roughly the 8th-12th best team in America, which feels about right for a program that finished outside the tournament last year and is still rebuilding its roster. The real money here is tracking whether their transfer class and returning talent can gel by March, because 15 months is an eternity in college hoops and the current price doesn't adequately account for the variance in how different teams peak.

daily_take.headline: Arizona's March Momentum Trap, Why 27.6% Is Fool's Gold
daily_take.deck: Arizona just popped 220 basis points in 24 hours on the back of mid-season tournament noise and a few decent wins. The market is pricing in a Final Four trajectory that doesn't exist yet. Smart money is selling the hype here, not chasing the green arrow. At nearly 28%, Arizona is overvalued against a field where Kansas, Alabama, and Duke offer cleaner paths to cuts.
